item.title: SORN vehicle abandoned on our road, 30th April
item.description:
The vehicle has been written off and should not be on a public road. It looks in bad state with wing mirror hanging off and flat tyre. It needs to be removed

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Buckley Road, London

item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Resident of house is fly tipping, 30th April
item.description:
To Whom It May Concern, I am reporting an incident of fly-tipping that occurred late yesterday evening along the path bordered by wooded brambles. While walking my two dogs, Isabella and Louie, I observed a large adult male exit a nearby property (exact address unknown) and dispose of a plastic rubbish bag into the brambles. The bag split on impact, spilling visible household waste across the area. For personal safety reasons, I did not confront the individual. However, the conduct was clearly deliberate and unacceptable. I request the following actions: Arrange prompt clearance of the waste. Issue a formal notice to local residents reinforcing that fly-tipping is illegal and subject to enforcement. This presents an immediate health risk. One of my dogs has food intolerances, and the exposed waste increases the likelihood of ingestion. Please confirm when the site has been cleared and what steps will be taken to prevent recurrence.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A21, Bromley Common, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Household waste all iver pavement, 30th April
item.description:
Black bag of rubbish all over the footpath leading from Rancliffe Gardens to Westhorne Avenue. Appeared overnight

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westhorne Avenue, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big fly tip in bins area, 30th April
item.description:
There are two big sofas, a big wooden vanity with glass that has not been smashed, a big bookcase and two big wooden tubes. The truck picking this up needs to be pretty much empty or not everything will fit at once. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Paragon Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter build up, 30th April
item.description:
Lots of litter on Colyers Lane (only checked lower end towards North End Road). A very depressing walk to school.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Colyers Lane, North End, Greater London

item.title: Two door panels one made of glass and a metal railing, 30th April

Metal railing been there for weeks Doors only since yesterday

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Richborough Close, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent Safety Concern: Roundabout Becoming Accident Hotspot, 30th April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to raise an urgent concern regarding the roundabout located in our area, which is increasingly becoming a near-accident hotspot. Over recent weeks, I have personally witnessed multiple near misses involving vehicles navigating this roundabout. Drivers appear confused about right-of-way, and visibility issues—particularly during busy periods—seem to be contributing to dangerous situations. It is only a matter of time before a serious accident occurs, if one has not already happened. The current layout and traffic flow do not appear to adequately support safe use, especially given the volume of vehicles and the speed at which some approach the junction. This creates a highly risky environment for drivers, cyclists, and pedestrians alike. I strongly urge the council to investigate this matter as a priority and consider appropriate measures. These might include clearer signage, improved road markings, better lighting, or even a redesign of the roundabout if necessary. Prompt action could prevent a potentially serious incident. Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response and to seeing steps taken to improve safety in our community. Yours faithfully, Kledian murati

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Redriff Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Paper and clothes recycling bins full & flytip again, 30th April
